name: Michael's Braised Ribs
recipeIngredient: 4 cups All-purpose stock (or low-sodium chicken broth)
recipeInstructions:
------------
Braising Ribs:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Mix onions, carrots, celery, garlic, and tomato paste in a roasting pan large enough to hold the ribs in a single layer.
3. Lay the ribs on top and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
4. Add stock, bay leaves, and thyme.
5. Cover and place in the oven for 1 hour.
6. Remove from oven; set ribs aside to cool.
7. Strain braising liquid and discard vegetables.
8. To freeze ribs: Pour cooled liquid into a small freezer container and refrigerate until solid (1 hour).
9. Discard any congealed fat on surface of liquid.
10. Cover tightly and place in freezer for up to 3 months.
11. Wrap meat in plastic wrap and place in airtight bags or containers; freeze for up to 3 months.
Barbecue Ribs:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F or light charcoal grill.
2. Defrost braising liquid if previously frozen.
3. Combine sugar and vinegar in a 1-quart pot over medium heat on stove.
4. Cook until vinegar reduces and forms syrup with sugar (about 8 minutes).
5. Watch carefully; the syrup may darken suddenly.
6. Add rib braising liquid, then catsup, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, cloves, chili powder, and cayenne pepper.
7. Reduce to thick sauce-like consistency; remove from heat.
8. Cook ribs in a roasting pan covered with sauce for 30 minutes, basting every 10 minutes at 350°F.
Grilling Ribs:
1. Wait for coals to become white hot; pile them on one side of grill.
2. Place ribs on the cooler side of the grill and cover so smoke collects inside.
3. Cook for 30 minutes, basting with sauce every 10 minutes.
4. Remove ribs to a platter and serve with additional barbecue sauce on the side.
